steps.title: Use the triangle angle sum property
steps.content:
In any triangle, the sum of all three angles equals 180°. Given ∠R = 63°:

$$63° + ∠S + ∠T = 180°$$

Subtract 63° from both sides:
$$∠S + ∠T = 117°$$

steps.title: Determine valid values for ∠S
steps.content:
For triangle RST to exist, angle T must be positive, so ∠T > 0°. Since ∠S + ∠T = 117°, this means ∠S must be less than 117°.

Check each choice:
  • A) 116°: ∠T = 117° − 116° = 1° ✓ Valid
  • B) 118°: ∠T = 117° − 118° = −1° ✗ Negative angle, impossible
  • C) 126°: ∠T = 117° − 126° = −9° ✗ Negative angle, impossible
  • D) 180°: ∠T = 117° − 180° = −63° ✗ Negative angle, impossible

That matches choice A — A is correct.
